title: Security Officer
how_to_become: Apply for a vacancy on the NHS recruitment website.
description: NHSScotland security officers maintain a safe and secure environment for patients, visitors, and staff within NHS facilities. They play a crucial role in preventing and responding to security incidents, protecting NHS assets, and promoting the overall wellbeing of individuals within healthcare settings.
role_overview: As a security officer, you'll help ensure hospitals and other NHS sites are safe and accessible for patients, visitors, and staff. The role is wide and varied, requiring calm performance under pressure.

entry_requirements.overview: A good standard of education is needed. Useful qualifications include IOSH or NEBOSH health and safety qualifications, SIA-accredited training, and a full UK driving licence.
useful_qualifications: IOSH or NEBOSH health and safety qualifications

learning_and_development.overview: Training in systems, policies, procedures, and equipment is required throughout your career. Some roles involving a helipad require Rescue and Fire Fighting Service training with the Civil Aviation Authority (annual refresher required).

chunks.text: To become a security officer in the NHS, apply for a vacancy on the NHS recruitment website.
NHSScotland security officers maintain safe and secure environments for patients, visitors, and staff, preventing and responding to security incidents and protecting NHS assets.

Useful school subjects include English, Maths, and Administration and IT. Useful qualifications include IOSH or NEBOSH health and safety, SIA-accredited training, full UK driving licence, and customer service experience.
With qualifications and experience, security officers can progress to security supervisor or security manager.
